content.text: *"Do to others as you would have them do to you."*{Luke 6:31}{Symmetry is the key that unlocks the door to many mathematical truths.}{Hermann Weyl}

Relations can have various structural properties that determine their 
behavior.

content.title: Reflexive
content.content:
A relation $R$ on $A$ is **reflexive** if $aRa$ 
for all $a \in A$.

Every element is related to itself.

content.title: Symmetric
content.content:
A relation $R$ is **symmetric** if $aRb$ implies 
$bRa$ for all $a, b$.

If $a$ is related to $b$, then $b$ is related to $a$.

content.title: Antisymmetric
content.content:
A relation $R$ is **antisymmetric** if $aRb$ 
and $bRa$ imply $a = b$.

Distinct elements cannot both be related to each other.

content.title: Transitive
content.content:
A relation $R$ is **transitive** if $aRb$ and 
$bRc$ imply $aRc$.

Relations "chain" together.

content.title: Properties of common relations
content.problem:
| Relation | Refl. | Sym. | Antisym. | Trans. |
| $=$ on $\R$ | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ |
| $<$ on $\R$ | $\times$ | $\times$ | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ |
| $\leq$ on $\R$ | $\checkmark$ | $\times$ | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ |
| $\mid$ on $\Z^+$ | $\checkmark$ | $\times$ | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ |
| "sibling of" | $\checkmark$ | $\checkmark$ | $\times$ | $\times$ |

items: Symmetric: edges come in pairs (both directions)
Antisymmetric: no two-way edges between distinct vertices
Transitive: if paths $a \to b \to c$ exist, so does $a \to c$

content.title: Checking properties
content.problem:
$R = \{(1,1), (1,2), (2,1), (2,2)\}$ on $\{1, 2\}$.

Reflexive: $1R1$ and $2R2$ $\checkmark$

Symmetric: $1R2$ and $2R1$ $\checkmark$

Transitive: $1R2$ and $2R1$ give $1R1$ $\checkmark$; all chains check out $\checkmark$

This relation is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive!

content.title: Symmetric vs. Antisymmetric
content.content:
These properties are *not* opposites! A relation can be:

- Both (equality is symmetric and antisymmetric)

- Neither ("is a sibling of" on a set with $> 1$ member)

marginNotes.text:
If you really keep the royal law found in 
Scripture, "Love your neighbor as yourself," you are doing right.
